# Implementation Journal — Entropy Protocol

_Append-only. One entry per session or significant decision. Each entry must reference a canonical document._

---

## 2026-05-01 — Phase 1 Bootstrap

**What happened:** Phase 1 governance package generated by Strategist. Architecture, spec, task graph, CODEX_PROMPT, Implementation Contract, and audit prompts all initialized.

**Key decisions made this session:**
- All Capability Profiles set to OFF — application is deterministic; AI used only at development level (D-001)
- Runtime T1 selected for CI environment; local workstation uses local services (D-002)
- Governance Standard: research-governance blast radius is recoverable in v1 (D-003)
- T16 (SimBroker Fill Engine), T17 (Calibration Interface), T18 (IS/OOS Splitter), T19 (Leakage Detection), T20 (Walk-Forward Runner), T21 (P&L Attribution), T22 (P1/P3 State Machine) marked as heavy tasks

**Open blockers from PROJECT_BRIEF.md (carry into implementation):**
- Harvey-Liu method package needs formula-level reproducibility and worked examples (-> T23 stub)
- Sharpe CI method needs derivation and validation examples (-> T23 stub)
- P4 weekly regime algorithm not independently reproducible (-> out of scope Phase 0 v1)
- Purge/embargo formula incomplete (-> T18 must document formula assumption and test boundary)
- K3/N_eff estimator must be locked across docs and implementation (-> T23)
- P1 circuit-breaker synthetic test suite must be enumerated (-> T22)

**What the next agent must know:**
- T01 starts from scratch — no implementation code exists yet
- existing docs/ directory has pre-bootstrap documentation that should be treated as canonical background reference (docs/core/PROTOCOL_SPEC.md, docs/core/CHARTER.md, docs/core/GLOSSARY.md) but is NOT implementation code
- Phase 0 success = all T01-T24 passing with tests and machine-checkable evidence; no OOS claims before T19 leakage check passes on a registered run

---

## 2026-05-01 — Language Escalation Policy Added

**What happened:** Added a formal language escalation policy after the Phase 1 audit passed.

**Key decision made this session:**
- Python remains the only active Phase 0/1 implementation language.
- Rust, Go, C, C++, FFI, native extensions, or extra runtime services require a measured bottleneck, reproducible benchmark, ADR, CI/task updates, and explicit human approval before any code is added (D-009).

**What the next agent must know:**
- Do not introduce non-Python files or toolchains during T01-T24 as an incidental implementation detail.
- If performance becomes a real blocker, first produce the benchmark evidence and ADR required by `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md §Language Escalation Policy` and `docs/IMPLEMENTATION_CONTRACT.md §Language Escalation Control`.
